review.summary: Implementation review must use WebEnvoy's repository-owned review standard.
Review must honor WebEnvoy's root repository rules and delivery discipline.
Review must honor WebEnvoy's docs/dev workflow, FR, worktree, and maturity rules.

merge_ready.summary: Merge-ready must consume WebEnvoy's guardian, GitHub checks, PR metadata, live evidence, and integration gate rules.
Merge-ready must preserve WebEnvoy's PR metadata contract, including integration_check and gate_applicability.

closeout.summary: Closeout must honor WebEnvoy's issue closing semantics, live evidence freshness, and blocker split rules.
Closeout must keep GitHub Issues and Projects as the progress truth and avoid local backlog truth.

specialized_gates.summary: PRs that claim real browser, live read/write, or formal closeout evidence must satisfy WebEnvoy's live evidence gate.
PRs touching cross-repo shared contracts or integration governance must satisfy WebEnvoy's integration_check metadata.
Formal FR and architecture changes must pass WebEnvoy's repository-owned spec review rules.

fields.summary: WebEnvoy PR metadata block for cross-repo shared contract and integration readiness.
WebEnvoy PR metadata block for formal spec review, governance lanes, and live evidence gate applicability.
WebEnvoy PR metadata block for latest-head fresh live evidence when the live evidence gate applies.
WebEnvoy PR metadata block for closeout issue type, readiness, evidence freshness, fallback limits, and blocker split handling.
Formal WebEnvoy FR suites live under docs/dev/specs/FR-XXXX-* and remain repo-owned truth.
GitHub Issues and Projects remain the authoritative progress and backlog truth.
